entry.title: Email Notifications in SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation
entry.content:
Elevate Your Email Notification Strategy 

Unlock the full potential of your SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation experience by mastering the art of email notifications. Figure out how you can supercharge your communication strategy.

Using the link embedded in the email notification, your end users can instantly navigate to the relevant items and easily handle their daily tasks.

Example Email Notification

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Two Powerful Options for Email Notifications

1. Seamless Integration with SAP Cloud ALM’s Built-in Service
  • Effortless Setup: No admin-level configuration required.
  • Professional Branding: Emails are sent under the name SAP Cloud ALM Notifications from the address sapcloudalm@notifications.sap.com.
  • Instant Activation: Start sending notifications immediately without any additional setup.
2. Leverage Your Own Email Infrastructure
  • Full Control: Use your own email server to customize the sender address.
  • Enhanced Security: Maintain control over your email infrastructure.
  • Simple Configuration: Set up an SMTP mail destination in your SAP BTP subaccount for SAP Cloud ALM. Follow the steps in Configuring an SMTP Mail Destination, and your notifications will be routed through your own server. 

Note: OAuth2 Client Credentials for Authentication supported as of now.

If that destination is configured, then the notification emails will only be sent via your own email server.

For detailed information regarding configuration you can check the Help Page - Configuring an SMTP Mail Destination.

Empower Users with Activation of Email Notifications

No matter which method you choose, users have the power to tailor their notification preferences:

  • Opt-In Flexibility: Unlike in-app notifications, email notifications are opt-in, giving users complete control.
  • User-Specific Settings: Each user can individually select which types of notifications should trigger an email. Administrators cannot mass-activate these settings, ensuring personalized experiences.

How to Activate Email Notifications

1. Access Your Profile: Click on your user profile in the top-right corner of the SAP Cloud ALM launchpad.
2. Navigate to Settings: Choose Settings > Notifications.
3. Customize Your Preferences: Check the Email flags for the notification types you want to receive emails for.

Note: You can only select notification types that are relevant to you. For instance, if you’ve never been assigned to a task, you won’t see this notification type in the settings and can’t enable email notifications for it.

Notification Settings

Following Notification Types are available

  • Defects: assigned to you

  • Features: assigned to you
  • Projects: New updates
  • Requirements: assigned to you
  • Requirements: new comment
  • Tasks: assigned to you
  • User management: open authorization requests

Wrap-Up

By harnessing these powerful options, you can ensure that critical updates and notifications are delivered directly to your inbox, keeping you informed and proactive. Choose the method that best fits your needs and take control of your SAP Cloud ALM notifications today!

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Learning - can an admin deactivate mor...
entry.content:
Hi @DavidRaykovitz, 

there is no connector for the instructor, but the import Tool (System Administration -> System Management Tools -> Import Data) provides this capability. When viewing the template, you have a column for "Active", which is required. 

When uploading, you can use Import Option -> Update and you should be able to deactivate the accounts. 

The hard part is to extract the data, but you can build a quick report via stories you can use it as a foundation. 

Cheers
Marc

entry.title: New Flexible Enterprise Architecture Training Starting in September
entry.content:
As organizations continue to shift more of their workload to the cloud, the role of the enterprise architect is becoming increasingly vital. Bridging the gap between business and technology, enterprise architects ensure companies can reach their desired business outcomes through the effective use of technology. Recent numbers prove the demand for this role: It was ranked as the number one best career on Glassdoor recently – highlighting high job satisfaction and a competitive salary. 

Recognizing the advantages that this role can offer both individuals and organizations, SAP has a long tradition of delivering best-in-class enterprise architecture training. However, many of you asked for a more flexible format to learn these skills – and we’re happy to share that a new offering is now available for enterprise architects: 

Introducing a new cohort-based, blended training 

Get ready to level up your game with our new cohort-based blended enterprise architect learning! Combining the flexibility of self-paced learning on the SAP Learning site with interactive and fun case study training in a series of live sessions, you can now get the best of both worlds to prepare for your SAP Enterprise Architect Certification. 

Sounds good? Here’s what you need to know: 

To get started, head over to the SAP Learning site and register for one series of live sessions. Each series consists of six individual sessions that take about 2,5 hours each. The first cohorts will start on September 16 with two time slots available to serve your personal time zone. You don’t have time in September? Don’t worry – further cohorts will start in early November and registration is already open. 

First cohort starts in September 

Once you’re registered, all you need to do is wait for your kick-off event. The weekly sessions will be moderated and guided by an experienced speaker and delivered via Zoom. The series of live sessions will be based on a case study and built on each other – so make sure you don’t miss a session! 

Working through the units within the learning journey is essential as preparation for the sessions with the speaker. In the sessions with the trainer, the participants work on practical examples in a case study. The speaker does not teach any theory; this is taught in the learning journey “Exploring the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework Foundation”. 

On the days where no live session takes place, you can deepen your knowledge with the new learning journey “Exploring the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework Foundation”. Available mid-September, the course will teach you how the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework is structured and how you can tailor it to your needs. After finishing the five units, you’ll be able to create an architecture vision of capabilities and business values, define a target business architecture to achieve business goals, and more. In just under 16 hours, this course will deepen your good understanding of business strategy, organization, and process concepts. 

Schedule your certification exam individually 

Your SAP Certification exam can then be scheduled individually after the last live session has taken place and your learning journey has been completed – so only five weeks after you started training! And of course, sharing your new badge as SAP Certified Professional - SAP Enterprise Architect is highly recommended to demonstrate your latest and comprehensive understanding of SAP solutions to help businesses use technology to meet their visions. 

Let’s talk about prerequisites 

To make sure you can make the most out of this cohort-based, blended training, the learning journey and live sessions are designed for architects with an intermediate proficiency level who possess TOGAF (The Open Group Architecture Framework) foundation knowledge, are TOGAF-certified and have a good understanding of IT strategy, applications, data, and technology concepts. Please note that this offering is included in a subscription to the enhanced SAP Learning Hub only.  

Sign up for the series of live sessions today and get ready to bring out your best with a new, flexible enterprise architecture training!

entry.title: SAP Community Meetup Kolkata
entry.content:
Connecting Beyond Screens: Embracing Your Local SAP Community

In the bustling heart of Kolkata, an exciting opportunity awaits for professionals immersed in the world of SAP systems. On the 17th of August 2024, at the prestigious Biswa Bangla Convention Centre, SAP enthusiasts will gather for an enriching community-driven event powered by Innoverv Team. Hosted by SAP Community member x Innoverv, this event promises to be a cornerstone in fostering local connections and expanding professional horizons.

Event Overview

Event Type: SAP Community meet up Kolkata
Duration: 4 hours (open-ended)
Location: BiswaBangla Convention Centre, Kolkata
Event Size: Approximately 500 participants
Host: SAP Community member x Innoverv
Day / Time: 17th August, 2024 from 9am to 1pm followed by Food & networking

What to Expect

This gathering is more than just a networking event; it’s a chance to engage face-to-face with fellow SAP professionals, share experiences, and explore potential collaborations. Whether you are a seasoned SAP expert or just starting your journey in SAP systems, this event welcomes all who are eager to connect and learn.

Event Components / Agenda

Networking Session: Begin with informal networking over refreshments, creating an atmosphere conducive to building new relationships.

Panel Discussion: Engage in a lively discussion led by Mr. Sourav Ganguly and Innoverv CEO, Partha Chakraborty, SAP Maestro, where you can ask questions, share insights, and gain valuable perspectives.

Work Opportunities: Discover career possibilities and partnership prospects with Accenture, our esteemed partner at the event.

Community Engagement: Join the  Kolkata - SAP Community  group to stay updated on future events and opportunities for involvement. 

Future Involvement: Learn how you can contribute to organizing or volunteering for upcoming SAP community events drop email to info@sitkolkata.org or connect by commenting in this post  Come Say Hello! What do you plan to learn or upski... - SAP Community.

Why Attend?

Whether you’re looking to expand your professional network, explore career opportunities, or simply connect with like-minded individuals passionate about SAP, this event offers a platform to achieve your goals. It’s a unique chance to move beyond virtual interactions and build meaningful connections in person.

How to Join

Interested in being part of this vibrant gathering? Mark your calendar for the 17th of August, 2024, and prepare to immerse yourself in an afternoon of learning and networking. While RSVPs are encouraged for planning purposes. 

Conclusion

Don’t miss out on this invaluable opportunity to engage with your local SAP community, gain insights from industry experts, and pave the way for future collaborations. Join us at the BiswaBangla Convention Centre on the 17th of August, 2024, and be part of a thriving network of SAP professionals dedicated to growth and innovation.

Please connect if you are interested in future local events organizing or volunteering with us. Let’s make this event a memorable milestone in our collective journey of SAP excellence!

(Please note that event details are subject to change. Refer to official SAP Community channels for the most current information and registration guidelines.)

#sapcommunity #kolkata #sitKol #meetup #networking #career #job

entry.title: Re: Partial scrap confirmation in DM, Quantity Confirmati...
entry.content:
Hi Rahul,

Thank you for your detailed answer. We have already tried that approach and we have enabled the quantity field.

Let's say the target quantity is 20 pieces and we type in 5 in the enabled quantity field. Then after clicking the Start button on the POD, that 5 pieces will be marked as "in progress". In this case if I log a nonconformance I can scrap only that 5 pieces together. What if I would like the log an NC only for one piece? What am I doing wrong?

Since the 5 pieces is already "in progress", all I can do is to split the SFC to 1 piece and 4 pieces. However the production workers would find that approach a bit to complicated.

Do you know a simpler solution, where the worker doesn't have to do that many clicks on the POD?

Thank you and Best Regards,
Oliver

entry.title: Re: how to transfer Site master with BP from S4HANA to ECC
entry.content:
Hi,

When the source system is S/4 Retail, and the target system is ECC Retail, you have two options as well.

1) simply using the PULL process in WBTIMEX (in ECC) - this does not need much explanation, I think

2) via DRFOUT, but here the BP is not transferred (this is a must), only the customer/vendor and address data, because in ECC the customer/vendor assigned to a site cannot be part of a BP.

I strongly recommend you to check the PDF attached SAP note 2310884, as it is the most detailed guide for site coversion/distribution released ever by SAP, containing above scenarios, too.

Best regards,
Andras

entry.title: SAP Business Network for Logistics 2407 Release – What’s New?
entry.content:
Another month has passed and the 2407 release went live over the past weekend. This blog post gives you an overview of the month’s release highlights.

The blog is intended for shippers and solution owners, carriers, and data providers as well as sales and presales colleagues who already have a detailed understanding of SAP Business Network for Logistics. 

Cross SAP Business Network for Logistics

Integration platform migration

We are making further progress with regards to integration platform migration to the SAP BTP multi-cloud environment. It is now available on US20 and EU10 landscapes, both for test and productive systems.

Please find the published documentation related to migration of SAP BN4L integration platform below:

  • Announcement Blog
  • Integration Platform Migration Guide for Premium Users (shippers and solution owners)
  • Integration Platform Migration Guide for Freemium Users (carriers and data contributors)
  • Migration page published in Administration Guide for premium customers (shippers and solution owners) and freemium customers (carriers and data contributors)

To support the migration the following UI changes were implemented in the ‘System Connections’ app for shippers and solution owners:

  • ‘Connections’:
    • The single connections are now grouped by connection type.
    • From the banner, you can navigate to the Integration Platform Migration Guide for Premium Users (link provided above).
  • ‘Inbound to Network’: We introduced a new field with the timestamp of the last update and added a ‘Refresh’ icon because the certificate update is now an asynchronous activity.

The following inbound connection statuses are now available: ‘In Progress’ and ‘Error’.

  • ‘Manage Cloud Connector Details’: The ‘SAP Users’ heading was renamed as ‘SAP User ID or Email ID’
  • ‘Outbound from Network’: For active authentication type Cloud Connector with Basic Authentication, the ‘SAP User ID’ drop down menu was renamed as ‘Email ID’ for new connections. The values are filtered by Email ID.
  • ‘Manage Cloud Connection Details’:
    • There are separate tabs for Locations and SAP Users (instead of two tables on the same page)
    • The ‘SAP Users’ column was renamed as ‘SAP User ID or Email ID’.
  • Further, the following UI changes were implemented in the ‘System Connections’ app for carriers and data contributors:
  • The ‘Configure Inbound Connections’ link was renamed as ‘Connection Settings’ because you can now also make settings for outbound connections.
  • Under ‘Connection Settings’, we now have separate sections for inbound and outbound.
  • In the new Integration Platform dropdown list, you can choose from the following values:
    • Neo (old) – set per default
    • Multi-Cloud (new)

 

Availability of platform for SAP Business Network Business Profile  

The platform is now available on EU10 as well as US 20 data centers.

 

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration

Contracting: external planning enhancements

When the shipper changes the carrier information in their external system, the system updates SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration with the updated carrier details. This cancels the freight unit for the old carrier and a freight unit is created for the new carrier.

 

Contracting: new fields in ‘Freight Order for Confirmation’ application

Carriers have several new fields in the ‘Freight Order for Confirmation’ worklist and detail view.

The first group of new fields are:

  • ‘Normalized Quantity’ and
  • ‘Additional Normalized Quantity’

 In addition, another group of new fields is now available in the filter and worklist area:

  • ‘Requested Loading Start Date Time’
  • ‘Requested Loading End Date Time’
  • ‘Acceptable Loading Start Date Time’
  • ‘Acceptable Loading End Date Time’
  • ‘Requested Loading Duration’

 

SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

Special character ‘+’ now supported in API

The read and write service APIs now support the special character “+”. As a result, information that contains this character can be processed and interpreted successfully, for example, mobile number.

Retention settings now only on ‘tracked process’

The retention settings are now defined on tracked processes only, rather than on both tracked processes and events. This improves the performance and user experience when sending data to the blocking store in SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace.

Note that the retention settings of existing events are still valid.

 

Track Shipments app: display ‘Actual Departure Time’

On the worklist page of the Track Shipments app, you can now filter shipments by ‘Actual Departure Time’ and see this information in the corresponding column of the worklist. On the details page, you can also display the field with the Adapt UI function.

 

 

Please share and amplify our new features!

---------------------

The above summarizes the key innovations for 2407. We hope you enjoy the new capabilities. Thanks for reading the blog post and stay tuned! 

A full overview of the 2407 release can be found in the following link.

To learn more about SAP Business Network for Logistics visit SAP - Business Network for Logistics.

For further questions and feedback related to the blog, please check the Q&A area and feel free to post your questions in the link with the tag SAP Business Network for Logistics.

entry.title: SAP Training und Change Kaffee Ecke: Widerstände im Change – Ursachen, Formen und möglicher Umgang
entry.content:
Am 18.7. diskutierten wir in der SAP Training und Change Kaffee-Ecke über Widerstände bei organisatorischen Veränderungen. Prof. Dr. Jürgen Radel von der HTW Berlin leitete das Thema ein und beantwortete Fragen wie: Wann verändern sich Menschen und wie kann man nachhaltigen Wandel herbeiführen?

Check-in und erste Diskussion

Wir begannen mit der Frage: "Was war der letzte Widerstand gegen eine Veränderung, den Du gespürt hast und was könnten Gründe dafür sein?" Antworten reichten von "Kurzarbeit trotz hoher Arbeitslast" bis "Last Moment of Widerstand: Fiori NEIN!". Im Anschluss startete Prof. Radel mit seinem Impuls.

Intro: Lust oder Schmerz - Wann verändern sich Menschen?

Laut Jürgen Radel verändern sich Menschen entweder aus Lust oder Schmerz. Ein anschauliches Beispiel nannte er aus den Anfängen der Pandemie und der Frage, ob Studierende bei Online-Vorlesungen gezwungen werden sollten, ihre Kameras einzuschalten – eine Veränderung, die sofort starken Widerstand hervorgerufen hat und massiven Stress bedeutete.

Widerstand als solcher ist für Jürgen zunächst positiv, da es sich um Energie handelt, die aber nur in eine andere Richtung geht als geplant / gewünscht.

Jürgen ist der Ansicht, dass im Kontext “Change managen” zwei Probleme zu berücksichtigen sind: Offensichtlich läuft es im Moment nicht so gut oder nicht mehr gut, deswegen gibt es einen Change. Das zweite Problem ist, dass betroffene Personen nicht das gewünschte veränderte Verhalten zeigen, so dass sie gemanagt werden müssen. Dies wiederum führt zu Widerständen, denn im Zweifel will verändert werden

Eine weitere Frage, die Jürgen in diesem Kontext in den Raum warf, war, ab wann Managen Manipulation ist und ob Manipulation schlecht ist sowie die Frage, was Manipulation von Projektmanagement unterscheidet.

Jürgen meinte, dass es mindestens drei Hauptprobleme gibt, die Widerstand in Organisationen auslösen:

  1. Sprung nach vorne
  2. Zustimmung als Illusion
  3. Individuen sind irrelevant – der Bedeutungsverlust des Individuums

 

  1. Problem: Sprung nach vorne

Veränderungsprojekte konzentrieren sich oft zu sehr auf die Zukunft und schneiden die Vergangenheit abrupt ab oder werden diese als negativ. Dies führt zu einem emotionalen Ungleichgewicht, da Menschen keine Gelegenheit haben, sich von alten Praktiken (z. B. Excel vs. ERP) zu verabschieden. In Changeprojekten bzw. -prozessen steht immer die Zukunft, die Vision im Vordergrund.

Ein wesentlicher Aspekt des erfolgreichen Change Managements ist es, genug Zeit für das Loslassen, den Übergang und die emotionale Übergangsphase zu investieren. Methoden wie das Verwenden von Ritualen und das Würdigen und Abschließen vergangener Prozesse können hier hilfreich sein.

 

  1. Problem: Zustimmung als Illusion

Auch wenn scheinbar alle Beteiligten zustimmen, kann dies eine Illusion sein. Jürgen betonte, wie “trainiert” wir als Mitarbeitende im Kontext von Hierarchien und Zwischentönen sind. Oft werden Annahmen und Fantasien seiner Ansicht nach nicht hinterfragt und dementsprechend nicht aufgeklärt.

Jürgen Radel ging auf den Begriff "Violent Politeness" (“gewaltsame Freundlichkeit”) ein – darunter ist zu verstehen, dass die die wahre Haltung der Beteiligten oft im Verborgenen bleibt.. Violent Politeness ist, wenn man aus Angst, z. B. davor unhöflich zu erscheinen, wichtige Dinge nicht anspricht und dadurch größere Probleme verursacht.

Eine Form von "Violent Politeness" ist die Immunität: Beteiligte sind vermeintlich dem Change gegenüber offen eingestellt und unterstützen ihn (und glauben das auch selber), sind aber immun gegenüber der Veränderung, da sie Commitments haben, die dazu konträr laufen -sogenannte versteckte Competing Commitments.

Ein weiterer Punkt, den Jürgen Radel in dem Kontext nannte sind sog. Seitenwetten. Diese sind Zusagen, die Beteiligte woanders gemacht haben und die dazu führen, dass sie ein Vorhaben nicht unterstützen – hier ist die Aufgabe, diese Verpflichtungen zu erkennen.

Eine weitere Form von "Violent Politeness" ist die Organisational Silence - erlernte Hilflosigkeit (“es macht keinen Sinn, etwas zu sagen, da sich eh nichts ändert”), so dass die Organisation passiv wird. Das führt dazu, dass diese Beteiligten still sind und kein Feedback geben. Ein Grund dafür ist, laut Jürgen Radel, ein in Organisationen nicht aussprechbares Thema der inkompetenten Führungskräfte, die Vorhaben entscheiden. 

 

  1. Problem: Individuen sind irrelevant – Der Bedeutungsverlust des Individuums

Zunächst ging Jürgen Radel auf das Modell PRO ein: Person, Rolle und Organisation.

Gemäß Jürgen ist das Individuum im ersten Moment in der Organisation irrelevant - entscheidend ist die Rolle, die es einnimmt und über die das Individuum eine Verbindung zur Organisation bekommt.

Diese Rollen geben Halt, sind jedoch ein Fantasienkonstrukt und mit entsprechender Erwartungshaltung vom Rolleninhaber und der Organisation an die Rolle verbunden. Im unwahrscheinlichen Idealfall ist die Erwartungshaltung von beiden Seiten deckungsgleich.

Wenn Rollen übertragen bzw. Rollen angenommen werden, kann viel Intransparenz und Raum für Interpretation eröffnet werden.

Jürgen ging zudem darauf ein, was passiert, wenn die Organisation größer wird: die Rolle wird kleiner und irrelevanter. Wenn z. B. neue Prozesse eingeführt werden in IT-Projekten und Prozesse definiert werden, führt dies zu mehr Transparenz. Transparenz geht einher mit einer besseren Nachverfolgbarkeit und Messbarkeit von Aktivitäten.

In Veränderungsprozessen sind die Individuen in sog. Zonen unterwegs: Comfort Zone (“Rauskommen ist ganz ok”), irgendwann fängt die Discomfort Zone an (“da habe ich nicht für unterschrieben”), Panik Zone (Entscheidungen sind risikoavers und kurzfristig orientiert).

Das Leiden des Individuums an der Organisation

Der Wandel bringt oft Anpassungsstörungen mit sich. Organisationen müssen daher Mechanismen entwickeln, um Personen mit Anpassungsstörungen zu unterstützen. Jürgen Radel wies auf die wachsende Notwendigkeit professioneller Begleitung hin, besonders im Hinblick auf die steigende Anzahl psychischer Belastungen (siehe auch Jonathan Haidt – Anxious Generation (https://jonathanhaidt.com/anxious-generation/). Zudem ging Jürgen Radel darauf ein, dass dauernde Veränderung bzw. Dauerreiz nicht empfehlenswert ist, da die Betroffenen auch Zeit zum Heilen bzw. für eine Balance brauchen.

Wie können wir mit Widerständen umgehen?

Eine Empfehlung, wie der Wandel unterstützt werden kann, ist, Schlüsselpositionen zu identifizieren: wer sind die wichtigen Player mit Wissen, das schwer zu vermitteln ist und diese Personen individuell zu unterstützen.

Gegebenfalls auch darin zu unterstützen, Seitenwetten zu bedienen, um Stress zu mindern. Darüber hinaus gilt es, Betroffene zu Beteiligten zu machen, wobei sich oft die Frage stellt, WIE. Jürgen sprach in dem Kontext von Struktur geben, um Stress zu mindern.

Strukturgebung bietet den nötigen Halt und reduziert Stress in unsicheren Zeiten. Rituale und routinemäßige Meetings können ein Gefühl der Stabilität vermitteln. Jürgen Radel verwendet hier die Metapher der "gewaltlosen Struktur" – ein Balanceakt zwischen Führung und Kontrolle.

Die Herausforderung besteht darin, die Rollen so zu gestalten, dass sie sich mit den Bedürfnissen und Erwartungen der Organisation decken, ohne das Individuum emotional zu destabilisieren.

Als ultimo ratio erwähnte Jürgen Radel die Trennungskultur, wobei dabei immer zu klären ist, ob es um das Wollen oder können geht. Fehlendes Können kann man unterstützen, bei fehlendem Wollen sollte man die Trennung reflektieren.

Diskussion rund um Widerstände

Im Anschluss an den Impuls entstand eine rege Diskussion mit unterschiedlichsten Hinweisen und Ideen gerade auf die Frage hin, wie die Teilnehmenden mit Widerständen umgehen:

  • “Mir fällt dazu spontan der Ausruf eines ehemaligen Kollegen ein: "Oh Mann. Als ob wir hier nicht schon genug Veränderungen hätten. Jetzt gibt's auch noch 'ne neue Kaffeesorte. Können sie nicht wenigstens so eine Kleinigkeit mal unverändert lassen." In dem Zusammenhang wurde gerade Free Seeting, Scrum & DevOps und Open Office eingeführt. Plus diverse Org-Änderungen. Das der GF dann wirklich dafür sorgte, dass es vorübergehend wieder die alte Kaffeesorte gab, fand ich einen guten Schachzug.”
  • Change Readiness Analyse oder Impact Analyse sind Instrumente, in denen ich Widerstände erfassen und analysieren kann. Diese sollten eigentlich immer durchgeführt werden. Nicht zu sehr auf eine Gruppe konzentrieren, globale, übergreifende Change Readiness Analyse (beinhaltet Stakeholderanalyse) - dauerhaftes Stakeholdermanagement folgt daraus, mit der Analyse ist es nicht getan - etwas aus der Flugperspektive betrachten
  • Systeme erhalten sich erst einmal selbst und lassen sich von extern allenfalls - mit viel systemischem Know-How, Empathie und Fingerspitzengefühl "zärtlich verstören", plus: in der Auftragsklärung sind Hypothesen über die guten Gründe der "Geschickten" extrem wichtig, weil diese genau diese Steine in den Weg legen können
  • Bei uns gab es einmal eine "Supervision" mit "Brüllt doch mal soviel ihr wollt, lasst es raus"
  • Zum Thema Widerstände gab es auch die Meinung, dass die meisten Fehler bereits lange vor einem Change/Projektstart. Die Grundlagen für einen Changen werden oftmals vernachlässigt und  passieren und das "Fundament" nicht gebaut wird. Dazu gehören eine umfangreiche und wertige Stakeholder-Analyse, Sponsor-Onboarding und Coaching, richtiges Kick-Off (meist werden falsche Personen eingeladen und die richtigen gar nicht - erster großer Fehler), Visionserstellung (motiviert mich, erzählt mir ein Bild der Zukunft) und viele andere Dinge. Die Grundlagen für Widerstände werden also weit, weit vor dem eigentlichen Projekt gelegt.
  • Ein weiterer Fehler ist, dass sich super erfahrene Projektmanager nicht eingestehen, dass sie eben nicht unbedingt super erfahrene Change Practitioner sind.
  • Auftragsklärung sollte immer auch potentielle Widerstände abklopfen sowie die Frage, was will mein Auftraggeber? Ist es eine  Alibi Veranstaltung - wer ist wirklich mein Auftraggeber
  • Was hilft zur Trauerbewältigung? Vergangenes verabschieden: nicht in "Früher war alles besser" verfallen, Aufschreiben, was sie toll fanden in eine Box und packen zur Verabschiedung.

Literaturhinweise und weiterführende Links

  • Podcast mit Prof. Dr. Jürgen Radel zum Thema Widerstand https://podcast.opensap.info/education-newscast/2024/02/12/enc285-widerstand-gegenuber-veranderungen-erkennen-erklaren-und-angehen-mit-prof-dr-jurgen-radel/ 
  • Proxyprobleme: https://youtu.be/31ogvMW4gnw

Wer das SAP Training and Adoption Forum verpasst hat, findet die umfangreiche Summary inklusive aller Recordings und Slides hier https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-training-and-change-management/r%C3%BCckblick-auf-das-sap-training-und-change-forum-2024-mit-aufnahmen-folien/ba-p/13745905

Wir danken allen Teilnehmerinnen und Teilnehmern für die lebhafte Diskussion und freuen uns auf die nächste SAP Training und Change Kaffee Ecke. Zur Anmeldung geht es hier https://events.sap.com/de/sap-change-training/de/home

entry.title: [CPI-Groovy]: Compare Integer and remove XML node
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Requirement is to compare the dates, if the difference more than the specified days we need to discard the particular XML node for further processing. I have written groovy scripts for the same. Not sure why it is not working.

Any help or suggestions would be highly appreciated. 

Parameters:

interfaceLastRunTime = 2024-06-01T00:00:00
effDateDiff = 60
ManualRun= Yes
golive_date = 
Manual_Delta_LSRD = 2024-06-01T00:00:00
end_dt = 2024-07-31T00:00:00

 Groovy Script:

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;
import groovy.util.XmlSlurper;
import groovy.xml.MarkupBuilder;
import groovy.xml.XmlUtil;
import java.util.Date;
import java.text.SimpleDateFormat;
import java.util.Calendar;
import groovy.time.TimeCategory;
import java.util.GregorianCalendar;

def Message processData(Message message) {

    def body = message.getBody(java.io.Reader);
    def payload = new XmlSlurper().parse(body);

    map = message.getProperties();
    def lsrd = map.get("interfaceLastRunTime");
    int effDateDiff = map.get("effDateDiff").toInteger();
    def manual_run = map.get("ManualRun");
    golive_date = map.get("golive_date");
    Manual_Delta_LSRD = map.get("Manual_Delta_LSRD");
    end_dt = map.get("end_dt");
    
	if(lsrd == null || lsrd == "")
    {
       if(Manual_Delta_LSRD == null || Manual_Delta_LSRD == "")
    	{
        	lsrd = golive_date;
    	}
		lsrd = Manual_Delta_LSRD;
    }
    lsrd = lsrd.substring(0, 19);
    SimpleDateFormat sdf = new SimpleDateFormat("ddMMyyyy");
    SimpleDateFormat sdtf = new SimpleDateFormat("yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss"); 

    payload.Row.each {r ->
       def pid = r.PID.text();
       def jobinfo_stdt = r.jobinfo_stdt.text()
       
       if(jobinfo_stdt != null && jobinfo_stdt != "")
       {
           Date new_jobinfo_stdt = sdf.parse(jobinfo_stdt);
           Date new_LSRD = sdtf.parse(lsrd); 
           
            int dateDiffInDays = new_LSRD - new_jobinfo_stdt;
            dateDiffInDays=dateDiffInDays.toInteger()+1
            println dateDiffInDays
            println effDateDiff
           if(dateDiffInDays >= effDateDiff )
           {
               r.replaceNode{}
            //message.setBody(dateDiffInDays.toString())
            }
       }
    }       
    message.setBody(XmlUtil.serialize(payload));
    return message;
}

XML Input:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Records>
	<Row>
		<ACTION>CHANGE</ACTION>
		<PID>M123456</PID>
		<Last_Name/>
		<Last_Name_previous/>
		<First_Name/>
		<First_Name_previous/>
		<salutation/>
		<Gender/>
		<jobinfo_stdt>06042024</jobinfo_stdt>
		<jobinfo_stdt_previous/>
		<event>5</event>
		<event_previous/>
		<emplStatus>T</emplStatus>
		<FullPartTimeInd>true</FullPartTimeInd>
		<Permanent_Temporary>R</Permanent_Temporary>
		<recurring>
			<recurring_stdt>07052024</recurring_stdt>
			<recurring_value>1234</recurring_value>
			<recurring_value_previous/>
			<recurring_code>1111</recurring_code>
		</recurring>
		<recurring>
			<recurring_stdt>06042024</recurring_stdt>
			<recurring_value>42123</recurring_value>
			<recurring_value_previous/>
			<recurring_code>2222</recurring_code>
		</recurring>
	</Row>
	<Row>

entry.title: Customer Projects with T&E Billing & PoC-based Revenue Recognition
entry.content:
Relevant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.

Co-authored by Stefan Walz, Sven Nachtigall, Volkmar Zahn, Frank Hennrich, and Andreas Hammerschmidt.

 

Introduction

In a Time & Expense (T&E) scenario, billing and realized revenue are based on cost postings such as time and expense confirmations. With every time recording or expense posting, revenue is calculated based on the determined billing material and its sales price. If the maintained sales price is, for example, 100 EUR/h, the realized revenue for 1 hour time recording is also 100 EUR/h. A more detailed explanation on revenue recognition for T&E projects can be found in this blog.

However, not all T&E contracts allow full realization of revenue based on the maintained sales price. Contracts can also include a shared risk agreement, in which the supplier and customer agree on a certain planned effort for delivering a certain scope. If the actual efforts exceed the planned ones, the burden of the additional unplanned efforts are shared between the supplier and customer. As a result, the recognized revenue should not only consider delivered effort with normal sales prices, but should incorporate as well already known future additional efforts which can be charged with a reduced sales price only. While billing is carried out periodically based on efforts incurred to date, revenue accounting considers the progress of the project based on the cost-based percentage of completion.

This blog describes the steps and procedure to set up and manage such a customer project arrangement.

 

Solution architecture and capabilities

Costs and revenues should not be recognized as they occur but should consider future additional efforts at lower rates. underlying foundation for the cost-based Percentage of Completion (PoC) revenue recognition in a T&E scenario is the new financial planning version PRO03. This planning version, similar to the current ongoing planning version PRO02, is populated through the application job "Transfer Project Forecast to Finance". However, there is one significant difference with PRO03 compared to the others: Costs and revenues are distributed over periods; only one sum is assigned to the fiscal year in which the project end date falls.

Additionally, there's a new revenue recognition key, SPTMSR (Cost-Based PoC for DIP based T&E projects), which executes revenue recognition as described above. It is recommended to create a new contract material (for example, P005), from which this new revenue recognition key is derived. The configuration activity ‘Derivation of Recognition Key for Projects’ (SSCUI 102517) can be used to configure SPTMSR for the necessary company codes in combination with contract type PS02 (Time and Expenses) and the new contract material. The latter is then used as the material in the sales order item.

Project plan categories in Finance

The original project planning and agreed standard work for delivering a specific service include 100 hours of Senior Consultant work at a cost rate of 65 EUR/h and a sales price of 100 EUR/h. Although the project has not yet started, the project manager is already second work package that will be needed. As per the contract agreement with the customer, the sales price can only be 50 EUR/h for this package. This is accomplished through a specific price that is maintained for the customer project and work package.

Figure 1: Project planning in the Plan Customer Projects app

For billing, standard T&E with the contract material P005 is defined and both work packages are assigned to one billing item. As explained above, this new material would determine the new revenue recognition key SPTMSR.

Figure 2: Billing part of customer project in Plan Customer Projects app

The next step involves using the job application "Transfer Project Forecast to Finance" to populate the PRO03 plan category in the financial planning tables. If a forecast isn't maintained in the Review Customer Projects application, as illustrated in this example, the application job considers the ongoing plan.

Please note that the product (e.g., P005) can serve as a filter criterion in the job application. This method allows the transfer of forecast values solely for those WBS Elements (across various customer projects) that were configured for the use the revenue recognition key SPTMSR. This approach considerably reduces the runtime of the job application.

Figure 3: The application job Transfer Project Forecast to Finance

Figure 4: Plan category PRO03 in the Projects – Plan/Actual application

In this example scenario, the customer project is now up and running, and 10 hours have actually been booked on the first work package. Contrary to the standard T&E revenue recognition key, SPTM, these actual bookings lead to a recognized revenue of 954.55 EUR. When using SPTM, the recognized revenue would have been 1000 EUR.

𝑅𝑒𝑐𝑜𝑔𝑛𝑖𝑧𝑒𝑑 𝑅𝑒𝑣𝑒𝑛𝑢𝑒  = (𝐴𝑐𝑡𝑢𝑎𝑙 𝐶𝑜𝑠𝑡𝑠/𝑃𝑙𝑎𝑛𝑛𝑒𝑑 𝐶𝑜𝑠𝑡) ∗ 𝑃𝑙𝑎𝑛𝑛𝑒𝑑 𝑅𝑒𝑣𝑒𝑛𝑢𝑒

                                   = (650 EUR / 7,150 EUR) * 10,500 EUR

                                   = 954.55 EUR

Forecasted costs as well as forecasted revenue would be considered if maintained in the Review Customer Projects application.

Figure 5: Event-Based Revenue Recognition after posting 10h actuals

 

Additional considerations, especially for Finance

So far, planned revenues in revenue recognition have been calculated either based on T&E billing simulation (Time and Expenses, Usage Based Billing) or the billing plan (Fixed Price, Periodic Service). For the first time, the new method explained in this blog uses financial planning values from ACDOCP, including ETC/EAC values. This also leads to a different behaviour for repricing and the cap scenario, as outlined below.

The ETC/EAC values entered in the Review Customer Projects application are also considered when using SPTMSR This remains true even if you do not sustain forecasts; as a result, the application job is solely based on ongoing planning.

After running the application job "Transfer Project Forecast to Finance"

  1. Revenue recognition for already existing cost postings is revaluated based on the most current available EAC data in the revenue recognition run.
  2. The updated plan values for cost and revenue are displayed in the Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Projects application.

Although a cap can be defined on T&E contract types, its amount is ignored when the revenue recognition key SPTMSR is used. Additionally, write-offs will reduce the EAC revenues, but EAC cost will not be In contrast to the standard T&E revenue recognition, writing off cost items will not directly update the recognized revenue in EBRR.

In similar terms, a repricing run does not update unbilled revenue in EBRR for billing elements with SPTMSR. Instead, changes in sales rates must be reflected in updated PRO03 plan values. For this, the planned revenue in the work package needs to be recalculated before transferring the plan data to finance.

Please remember to always synchronize the project financial data with finance through the application job "Transfer Project Forecast to Finance" before running revenue recognition at the end of the period. This ensures up-to-date data in the financial planning version PRO03, even if no dedicated forecast is maintained.

One limitation to mention is that when cost postings already exist, the revenue recognition key can only be changed to "no revenue recognition key" and back.

 

Summary

T&E billing, in combination with PoC-based revenue recognition, allows for billing as per work performed (T&E), but realizes revenues based on Cost-based PoC. This provides an increased level of flexibility in financials. Additionally, manual efforts to adjust the accrued revenues can be avoided.

entry.title: Webinar Alert : Delivering Seamless S/4HANA Transformation for Oil and Gas
entry.content:
Delivering Seamless SAP S/4HANA Transformation for Oil and Gas Companies

Join our KaarTech webinar series if you are looking for ways to ease the pressure to modernize your ERP solution landscape
 
Are you grappling with legacy ERP systems that hinder growth and efficiency? Is data chaos and operational inefficiency keeping you awake at night? You're not alone. Many organizations face these challenges daily. That's why KaarTech invites you to join our exclusive webinar series where we delve into the transformative journey of a major oil and gas company.
 
Addressing Pain Points :
  • Overwhelmed by Legacy Systems: Learn how to overcome the hurdles posed by outdated ERP systems that fragment operations.
  • Data Chaos: Discover strategies to tackle data silos and integrity issues, crucial for accurate decision-making amidst diverse data sources.
  • Resistance to Change: Navigate through team resistance with proven tactics to make your S/4HANA transition smoother.
Why Attend? This 3-part series goes beyond theory to provide actionable insights and strategies tailored for SAP customers worldwide. Each session equips you with practical insights for a risk-free S/4HANA transformation from ERP core analysis to Tech Debt mitigation and change management.
 
Key Sessions Include:
  • Part 1 - Analyzing Your Existing SAP Investments and Assessing Technical Debt
  • Part 2 - Data Migration Strategies in Preparation for Transformation
  • Part 3 - Managing Your Organization's Resistance To Change
By the series end, you'll have:
  • A clear roadmap to improve your ERP core.
  • Strategies to optimize edge systems for enhanced supply chain efficiency.
  • Best practices for data management crucial for S/4HANA readiness.
  • Actionable insights into effective change management.
Join us for an insightful journey as we decode the path to SAP S/4HANA transformation. This isn't just another webinar; it's your guide to overcoming challenges and seizing opportunities in the digital era.
 
REGISTER NOW

 

 

SAP S/4HANA Oil, Gas, and Energy Digital Technologies Digital Transformation

_schema-version: 3.3.0

description: A simple CAP project.

parameters:

readiness-health-check-type: http

builder: npm
- name: StonemanPortal
type: approuter.nodejs
path: app/router
requires:

group: destinations

url: ~{srv-url}

- name: S4HANA_QA
group: destinations
properties:

name: ui5
url: https://ui5.sap.com

type: html5
path: app/stoneman

builder: custom

- npm run build:cf

entry.title: Re: Q) How to create API services and use them in SAP Dig...
entry.content:
Hi Wonpyo,

Please take a look at these blogs for an example of how to set up an external API:

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blogs-by-sap/integrating-microsoft-teams-with-sap-digital-manufacturing-cloud/ba-p/13499743

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blogs-by-members/external-api-web-service-integration-with-sap-digital-manufacturing/ba-p/13573720

In the first blog I explain how to create a webhook in microsoft teams (API) and then configure this in DM.

If you want to create you own API then what I normally do is write a Node JS application using VS Code and the Express framework and then push this into the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry service, it can then be registered in the service registry.

This tutorial show how to create and deploy a node JS app into cloud foundry https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/btp-cf-buildpacks-node-create.html 

Let me know if you need more details.

Thanks

Kevin

entry.title: Re: SAP Kyma environment ready for Production?
entry.content:
Hi Brian,

Could you please share the link of the tutorial to confirm?

I tried this one: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/deploy-nodejs-application-kyma.html and was able to access the application without any issue.

Here is my deployment descriptor:

---
apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v1beta1
kind: APIRule
metadata:
  labels:
    app: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
    release: multitenancy
  name: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
spec:
  gateway: kyma-gateway.kyma-system.svc.cluster.local
  host: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
  rules:
  - accessStrategies:
    - handler: allow
    methods:
    - GET
    - POST
    - PUT
    - PATCH
    - DELETE
    - HEAD
    path: /.*
  service:
    name: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
    port: 8080

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  labels:
    app: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
    release: multitenancy
  name: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
      release: multitenancy
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
        release: multitenancy
    spec:
      containers:
      - env:
        - name: PORT
          value: "8080"
        - name: TMPDIR
          value: /tmp
        image: gauravsap/multitenant-kyma-backend:v1  # replace with your Docker Hub account name
        name: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
        ports:
        - name: http
          containerPort: 8080
          protocol: TCP
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /
            port: http
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /
            port: http
        startupProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /
            port: http
          failureThreshold: 15
          periodSeconds: 2
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: 100m
            memory: 256M
          requests:
            cpu: 100m
            memory: 256M
        securityContext:
          allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
          capabilities:
            drop:
            - ALL
          privileged: false
          runAsNonRoot: true
          readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /tmp
          name: tmp
      volumes:
      - emptyDir: {}
        name: tmp

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: null
  labels:
    app: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
    release: multitenancy
  name: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
spec:
  ports:
  - port: 8080
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 8080
  selector:
    app: kyma-multitenant-node-multitenancy
    release: multitenancy
status:
  loadBalancer: {}

---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: null
  labels:
    release: multitenancy
  name: multitenancy
spec:
  ingress:
  - from:
    - namespaceSelector:
        matchLabels:
          name: istio-system
      podSelector:
        matchLabels:
          app: istio-ingressgateway
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      release: multitenancy
  policyTypes:
  - Ingress

I would like to investigate what could be the possible issue you are facing. I just deployed the image publicly and does not need image secret. You should be able to use it.

Regarding your question, Kyma is very much ready for production and use productively by various customers. Here, I suspect some wrong configuration or tutorial might have some issue.

Thanks,

Gaurav Abbi

entry.title: SAP Jam Collaboration sunset and which products does SAP offer as alternative solutions?
entry.content:
You are an SAP customer, who is still running SAP Jam Collaboration solutions? Then this blog post is relevant for you.

SAP Jam Collaboration was SAP’s enterprise collaboration platform and modern intranet solution for a long time. Then SAP made the strategic decision to exit the standalone collaboration area and focus on the Digital Workplace Experience market with products such as SAP Build Work Zone and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. As a result, SAP announced already in 2021 the maintenance mode and planned sunset of SAP Jam Collaboration for beginning of 2027. This includes all SAP Jam Collaboration editions: Basic, Advanced, Advanced Plus, and Enterprise.

What is the status of SAP Jam Collaboration in 2024?

  • All SAP Jam Collaboration editions are now in maintenance mode and were removed from the SAP pricelist in 2021.
  • While no new enhancements will be made, they continue to be fully supported in accordance with the customers’ cloud contract. It is planned to sunset SAP Jam in January 2027.
  • SAP Jam Collaboration is also not available for purchase for new customers, existing customers can continue to use their SAP Jam Collaboration products for the duration of their contract.
  • If you are currently licensed for SAP Jam Collaboration Advanced, Advanced Plus or Enterprise Editions, you can renew these subscriptions provided that the end date of the contract does not exceed December 2026.
  • If you have benefited from SAP Jam Collaboration Basic Edition, that was provided complimentary to your SAP SuccessFactors subscription, you are welcome to continue to use this until your contract end date, provided that the end date of the contract does not exceed December 2026.

Note: customers will not be able to use SAP Jam Collaboration after end of life of the solution, and all systems would be removed permanently after this date. Therefore it is very important for customers to plan a migration from SAP Jam Collaboration to the alternative products before December 2026 or by the end of their cloud contract.

Is there a legal successor for SAP Jam Collaboration? What is the recommended go-to product for SAP customers ?

There is no legal successor product for SAP Jam Collaboration editions. SAP has released alternative products with SAP Build Work Zone and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone – which provide many features known from SAP Jam Collaboration editions, but also offer a broad range of additional capabilities and innovative features. With these new products, various use cases like engaging employee experience platforms, modern intranets or integrated digital workplace scenarios can be implemented. 

Why is SAP investing into a unified workplace experience that increases engagement and productivity of end users?

When asking employees about the primary operational challenge that they encounter when trying to accomplish their day-to-day work activities, you will often get the answer that it is too hard for them find the data that they need, that they need too much time by switching between applications and finding the right applications to accomplish the tasks. The work experience is fragmented between multiple tools, tons of systems, repositories and applications. If we look at a typical SAP customer system landscape, the end users often need to access multiple SAP systems cloud and on-premise with multiple user experiences via desktop and mobile devices. SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Successfactors Work Zone offer a solution to this problem. They enable companies, IT departments and employees to improve this situation by bringing it all together and making it easier to consume. Both products deliver consistent and engaging user experiences across all channels, business processes, and applications.

What is SAP Build Work Zone?

SAP Build Work Zone empowers IT professionals and line of business experts to easily build and design engaging business sites for employees, partners and customers with intuitive drag-and-drop functionality. It connects to all systems (SAP and non-SAP) by creating a single access point to applications, processes, information and data on any device, from desktop or mobile, and it maximize team productivity by enabling guided experiences and knowledge sharing.

It is available in two editions:

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, provides a central access point to multiple SAP cloud & on-premise systems, integrating apps and providing customizations and extensions.
  • SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, provides advanced features for content management, web content, interactive and engaging workspaces, and integration with third party solutions. This image shows an example of a homepage created with the advanced edition:

For a migration from SAP Jam Collaboration to SAP Build Work Zone, the advanced edition is the correct migration path, as workspaces - the evolution of Jam groups - are only available in the advanced edition. 

See also: 

  • SAP Build Work Zone on SAP Community
  • SAP Build Work Zone on sap.com
  • FAQs

What is SAP Successfactors Work Zone and what is the difference to SAP Build Work Zone?

SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone is a comprehensive digital workplace solution offering a centralized hub for all work-related needs. From accessing information and completing tasks to learning, collaborating, and seeking support, SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone provides a single entry point for employees to easily navigate their work environment. SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone is a dedicated solution bundle with user-based subscription model. The package includes SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, plus several SAP BTP services (such as SAP Business Application Studio, Custom Domain). It includes the same feature set as the regular SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition.

All business content packages (such as HR) with pre-built UI Integration Cards, workflows (guided experiences) and workspace templates are also available in both commercial options: SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition as well as SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. More information: Commercial Models and Metering.

See also:
What is SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone? 
SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone on sap.com

Will there be tools available to support migration from SAP Jam Collaboration to SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone?

There are different manual and API-based options to export content, details can be found here:

  • How to export a complete SAP Jam group into a zip file
  • SAP Jam - How to backup or transfer content from one instance to another

These options will remain available until the end of customer contract for SAP Jam or at the latest until the final sunset date which is planned for January 2027.

Apart from this and in case customers decide to purchase and implement either SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone, an out-of-the-box migration and upgrade path is available. Details can be found in respective onboarding guides for SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. During the onboarding process a configuration wizard is available, which supports the creation of a service instance based on an existing SAP Jam tenant:

Why should customers migrate to SAP Build Work Zone?

(Note that this section is also relevant for SAP Successfactors Work Zone as it includes the same feature set as SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition.)

Features and Innovation
SAP Build Work Zone and especially the advanced edition will give you a lot of innovative features, which go far beyond the feature range that you know from SAP Jam Collaboration:

  • SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition includes most capabilities of SAP Jam Enterprise edition (and hence implicitly from the lower editions). It also provides many additional features not available in SAP Jam, like menu builder, UI Integration cards and guided experiences.
  • While admin areas and homepages are still part of the menu, a more flexible role-based menu builder with workpages is available. Workpages replace home & overview pages combining pre-built widgets (from SAP Jam) & Build Work Zone specific ability to add applications & UI Integration Cards.
  • Workspaces as part of SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition represent the evolution of SAP Jam groups with expanded scope and capabilities. Workspace offer simplified access control while optionally allowing access to external users. Unlike groups, workspaces can also offer a true read-only experience hiding all collaborative elements/features.
  • As you know from SAP Jam, SAP Build Work Zone also supports both uploaded (Office, PDF, Video, etc.) as well as natively created (blogs, wikis, workpages, etc.) content.
  • SAP Build Work Zone provides a user profile with profile-level content, feeds & settings and always provisioned using SAP Cloud Identity Services.
  • Joule integration is also planned for SAP Build Work Zone in 2024.  

Smooth migration path
Another motivation to start a migration project is the fact that the migration path from SAP Jam Collaboration to Work Zone is technically a smooth transition supported fully by tools. Also SAP IT went through this transition experience in 2022 with a large project covering 145 TB of data, 15 million documents and +150.000 Jam groups converted to workspaces in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition. November 25, 2022 was the date when SAP employees were provided a new digital collaborative workplace with SAP Build Work Zone replacing SAP Jam.
The cutover to SAP Build Work Zone involved remarkably little disruption for users. The "pure" technical migration took place only over one weekend. On Fridays, users still worked with SAP Jam groups. On Monday, they logged in and had SAP Build Work Zone workspaces in place, including all the content. Straight after the migration, several thousand groups (now: workspaces) were operating smoothly, letting employees complete tasks and interact with customers, partners, and other stakeholders.

Check out this video and learn from the SAP IT team how they empowered SAP employees with a modern digital workplace. You can also learn from the SAP migration project lead, Eduardo Antunes da Cunha, about his experiences with the technical migration to SAP Build Work Zone:

Watch the video!

What are the next steps?

  • Evaluate your use cases within SAP Jam Collaboration to determine your requirements.
  • Collaborate with your account team to discuss SAP's alternative solutions to meet your requirements and business needs.
  • Plan for your migration from SAP Jam Collaboration before December 2026 or by the end of your cloud contract (whichever is the earliest date). Do not miss the date: the migration support tools require that SAP Jam Collaboration is still available at customers’ site to support a migration to SAP Build Work Zone or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone.
  • If you would like to explore the extended capabilities of SAP Build Work Zone or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone, please reach out to your account team to strategize on your options.

entry.title: Re: Create button is Greyed-out in Manage incidents > Peo...
entry.content:
Hi @phaneendra_k ,

the "Create" button is greyed out due to lack of authorizations for creation of new Business Partners in S/4HANA. Of course, this behavior may be different, if you have enhancements in your system. If it was enabled, you would be able to create a new Business Partner record, which would then be linked to your incident record as an Injured Person (or any other role you choose).

You can also simply type the name of the person in the fields on the screen. 

 

Furthermore, if you want to add a new Injured Person, you have to use the "Add" button just above the list of Involved People.

Best regards, 
Georgi

entry.title: Re: secondary DB is not connecting on HANA studio 2.0, bu...
entry.content:
You cannot connect to a secondary HANA in an HSR setup with SQL at all (if the configuration is not Active/Active (Read Enabled)). So HANA Studio cannot connect via SQL either.  
If the HSR configuration is Active/Active (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/fe5fc53706a34048bf4a3a93a5d7c866.html?locale=en-US) the secondary SQL ports are open for read access only.
Not sure, whether HANA Studio is able to connect via SQL in this case.

if (doc1.OrderID > 0)

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP - Trigger workflow events upon save action in RAP Applications
entry.content:
Dears,

I have an interesting update to share. There is another option that serves the same purpose: the additional save functionality. Which uses the same implementation method save_modified.

The key difference between the unmanaged save and the additional save in the behavior definition is that the latter requires a persistent table.

Therefore, a persistent table can only be used with the additional save and not with the unmanaged save.

the new behavior definition accordingly:

managed implementation in class zbp_cds_pr_buyer_assign unique;

define behavior for ZCDS_PR_Buyer_Assign 
persistent table ZMM_PR_BUYER_BO
lock master
authorization master ( instance )
with additional save
{
  update ( features : instance, precheck );
  delete ( features : instance, precheck );

  field ( mandatory : create, readonly : update ) PurchaseRequisition;
  field ( mandatory : create ) buyer;


    field (readonly) PurchaseRequisitionItem;
    field (readonly) PurchaseRequisitionType;
    field (readonly) PurchasingDocumentTypeName;
    field (readonly) NumberOfItems;
    field (readonly) PurchasingDocumentSubtype;
    field (readonly) Currency;
    field (readonly) TotalNetAmount;
    field (readonly) Material;
    field (readonly) PurchaseRequisitionItemText;
    field (readonly) Plant;
    field (readonly) PurReqnReleaseStatus;
    field (readonly) PurchasingGroup;
    field (readonly) PurchasingGroupName;
    field (readonly) RequisitionerName;
    field (readonly) CreatedByUser;
    field (readonly) CreatedByFullName;


validation validateBuyer on save { field Buyer; create;update; }

}


Regards,

Abdelrahman Zaki

entry.title: Re: Runtime Exception ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY behaves differen...
entry.content:
Reproduced in 7.52 SP 04.

As far as I can see in the documentation of CORRESPONDING, it says that to avoid a duplicate exception (doesn't say what kind of exception it is), DISCARDING DUPLICATES is to be used.

Now, what should happen exactly if a duplicate happens, according to the documentation and is that true if I test in ABAP?

The documentation of CORRESPONDING says that the insertion behaves like MOVE-CORRESPONDING (for itabs).

The documentation of MOVE-CORRESPONDING says that "Finally, and if necessary, the table keys and associated table indexes are updated in the target table in accordance with the rules for insertions in internal tables. The corresponding exceptions are raised if uniqueness is violated."

It's still not very clear what exact rules are managed for "insertions in internal tables", it behaves differently for one line at a time and for one set of lines (LINES OF).

If you use MOVE-CORRESPONDING, you get ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY.

If you use INSERT one line without using LINES OF, the line is not inserted, SY-SUBRC is set to 4 and the program continues.

If you use INSERT LINES OF, you get ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY.

The documentation of INSERT, line_spec > LINES OF seems wrong to me: "If there is a conflict with an existing unique table key, a catchable exception is raised when inserting multiple lines from an internal table."

If you look at the general documentation of INSERT - itab, you finally obtain the correct rule lost at the bottom of the page: "Uncatchable Exceptions Cause: When inserting a set of lines, entries with an identical key were produced (the target table is defined by UNIQUE). Runtime error: ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY".

So, I guess that SAP could improve the documentation and clarify.

My tests:

DATA(itab3) = VALUE _hashed_table_type( ).
TRY.
    CASE 6.
      WHEN 1.
        " Runtime error ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY if duplicate
        itab3 = CORRESPONDING _hashed_table_type( itab1 ).
      WHEN 2.
        " Runtime error ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY if duplicate
        MOVE-CORRESPONDING itab1 TO itab3.
      WHEN 3.
        " Runtime error ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY if duplicate
        INSERT LINES OF itab1 INTO TABLE itab3.
      WHEN 4.
        " SY-SUBRC = 4 if duplicate
        INSERT VALUE #( id = 1 name = |Tamit| ) INTO TABLE itab3.
        INSERT VALUE #( id = 1 name = |Tamit| ) INTO TABLE itab3.
      WHEN 5.
        " CX_SY_ITAB_DUPLICATE_KEY if duplicate
        itab3 = VALUE _hashed_table_type( FOR <itab> IN itab1 ( CORRESPONDING #( <itab> ) ) ).
      WHEN 6.
        " Discard duplicate lines
        itab3 = CORRESPONDING _hashed_table_type( itab1 DISCARDING DUPLICATES ).
    ENDCASE.
  CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(error).
ENDTRY.

entry.title: SSO3 SPS SP02 (05/2017) -SUM - how to update the last SLSERVER02_12-80001957.SCA
entry.content:
Hello,

We installed and configured a SAP SSO3 System  1 year ago:

NW 7.5 Java SP25 - SAP SINGLE SIGN-ON 3.0  [ SPS SP02 (05/2017) ]

Checking SAP Portal Download area, I could see 3 newest components.
  SECURE LOGIN SERVER 3.0 SP02 from Patch 11 to Pach 12
  SSO AUTHENTICATION LIBRARY 3.0 from Patch 9 to Pach 12
  SAPCRYPTOLIBP from  Patch 8546 to Patch 8556

But when I run a new Maintenance Planner to update the Addon  SAP SINGLE SIGN-ON 3.0 , there is no new SPS, the last one is still  SPS SP02 (05/2017). And if I select it and go until the last step of Maintenance Planner, it is not updating the 3 components.

 

Question: Can I proceed as the following:

-To add the 3 Components last patch in my download basket

-To run Maintenance Planner to update the Addon  SAP SINGLE SIGN-ON 3.0

-On last Maintenance Planner, to select "Utilities -> Add from Download Basket" to add the 3 components to my SUM update XML Stack

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Down payment clearing Public Cloud

entry.title: Re: Landed Cost Functionality
entry.content:
Hi @JP ,

Yes, you could use Landed Costs to add 3rd party freight costs to your stock item.

Assuming the GRPO is already posted in the system, you just need to open the Landed Costs window, select the vendor, the broker (the carrier you'll have to pay in this case), input the freight costs on the costs tab (considering you have configured Landed Costs previously), and choose the allocation method. Post the document, run the Stock Audit Report, and see the costs added to the item(s).

Afterwards, you can create an AP invoice for the carrier, copying details from the very same Landed Cost document to finally post the freight charges.

SAP Documentation: SAP Help Portal

If you found this answer helpful, don’t forget to give kudos and mark it as an accepted solution if that is the case! Your feedback helps the entire community by making our knowledge base even bigger and valuable content more visible.👍

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Nice patch SAP! Revisiting your SAP BTP security measures after AI Core vulnerability fix
entry.content:
Dear community,

SAP recently fixed a critical vulnerability in the SAP AI Core service that could have allowed attackers to access sensitive data in the multi-tenant environment. This issue, dubbed "SAPwned", was responsibly disclosed and publicly shared on July 18 after it was patched. You can read more about it here.

Bottom line: SAP shows its commitment to security and timely patching of its cloud services. But remember, SAP BTP - like any cloud platform - is based on a shared responsibility model. That means you need to do your part to protect your data and applications too:

  • Pick secure authentication means (no Basic AUTH is not one of them!),
  • Be conscious that every endpoint exposed by SAP BTP like Microsoft365 lives on the Internet by design,
  • Scope Cloud Foundry + Kyma app access, and user roles to the minimum rights needed,
  • When using the popular” OAuth2 client credentials grant” with service keys rotate your secrets (at best automatically regularly)! Have your pick from app based solution like this, PowerShell module and blog on automatic cert renewal.
  • Establish a continuous process to harden your SAP cloud workloads. It is not a one stop shop.

Ever heard about “MFA fatigue”? Plain Multi-Factor-Authentication is not good enough anymore today. Additionally, enforce Conditional Access to SAP BTP service through integration the SAP ID Service or the SAP Identity Authentication Service with the corporate identity provider of your choice. See here how to do it with Microsoft Entra ID.

 

Second line of defense: Automatic detections based on the SAP Audit Log Service

Most of the BTP based services in the Cloud Foundry environment provided by SAP automatically write to the SAP Audit Log Service. Each service lists the standardized events that are propagated.

SAP has a nice video on the general workings of the SAP Audit Log Service on BTP.

This is a good start, but how useful are log entries that record a compromise if they are overlooked and hidden among countless normal entries?

I use the Microsoft Sentinel for SAP BTP solution - which went into General Availability state this week - as an example for running automatic detections via built-in analytic rules. Check out the alert “Failed access attempts across multiple Business Application Studio accounts” for instance. Password spray attack anyone?

Screenshot of Sentinel for SAP BTP solution content with out-of-the-box detections and workbooks

Once I have onboarded my subaccount (I named it SAP-AI-Core-playground), I can go wild on the ingested log entries, apply the threat intel functions, and built analytic rules. Let's browse the entries via the Kusto query language. The standard table SAPBTPAuditLog_CL holds all audit log info for your registered SAP BTP subaccounts:

Screenshot of simple KQL for SAP BTP

The Message contains the JSON payload BTP provides for each message as well as the involved BTP service identifier.

Looking at audit messages is nice, but you may go one step further by applying automatic action like blocking the SAP BTP users.

Below Screenshot shows the part of the process triggered by the included playbook. The SAP security team gets notified with evidence of the compromise, offering an approval option to block the user from a Microsoft Teams channel flow. Find more info here. Below screenshot shows the adaptive card with a trigger from SAP Business Suite. The same is possible with triggers coming from BTP too.

Screenshot of sap btp user block approval request to SAP security team on Microsoft Teams

 

The AI Core Service audit log entries alone are not useful

Threat protection-wise correlation with other signals in your company is required, because a single SAP AI Core event like “Successful retrieval of object store secret” does not tell you anything. See below a Kusto query working off the AI Core audit log info ingested by the Sentinel for SAP BTP solution.

Note: SAP publishes the available events for all the Cloud Foundry based services here. 

It identifies entries on my BTP subaccount related to AI Core activity and cross-references the IP address involved in the login and its country of origin. In my sample below I use the built-in function geo_info_from_ip_address() to learn if the BTP client remote address originated from Germany or not. Assumption here is that all my BTP developers are based there. Think about sanctioned countries lists etc.

 

 

 

 

//flag unexpected logins from countries other than Germany
let myBTPDevelopers = dynamic(['Germany']);
let login_messages = dynamic(['ClientAuthenticationSuccess','UserAuthenticationSuccess']);
SAPBTPAuditLog_CL 
| where SubaccountName == "SAP-AI-Core-playground"
| where Message has_any (login_messages)
| extend ip_ = tostring(Message.ip)
| extend country = geo_info_from_ip_address(ip_)['country']
| where country !in (myBTPDevelopers);

 

 

 

For a smoke test I teleported myself into the land of leprechauns🌈, steep cliffs, and mysterious celtic culture🍀 using an Azure VM. Marvel at the rule that identifies that mischieveous btp user!

Screenshot of found btp login from Ireland

 

The next sample uses the Threat Intelligence feature to verify if the BTP remote access can be traced back to a feed of known problematic IP indicators (e.g. a bot network). I maintained it on Sentinel on the Threat Management section using the IP known to BTP for my recent logins to the SAP AI Core service to trigger a result. In real life you would take the IPs from a threat intel feed of course. I don't have a bot net handy though😉.

Screenshot of Sentinel Threat Management experience

 That makes it available to my Kusto query as below. See below the screenshot of the result:

Screenshot of Kusto query result filtered by problematic IPs

 

 

 

//flag unexpected logins from IP indicators from Sentinel
let ips = ThreatIntelligenceIndicator
| distinct NetworkIP = tostring(NetworkIP);
let login_messages = dynamic(['ClientAuthenticationSuccess','UserAuthenticationSuccess']);
SAPBTPAuditLog_CL
| where SubaccountName == "SAP-AI-Core-playground"
| where Message contains "aicore" and Message has_any (login_messages)
| extend ip_ = trim(" ", replace(@"\s", "", replace(@"\r|\n|\t", "", tostring(Message.ip))))
| join kind=inner (
    ips
    | extend NetworkIP_ = trim(" ", replace(@"\s", "", replace(@"\r|\n|\t", "", tostring(NetworkIP))))
) on $left.ip_ == $right.NetworkIP_;

 

 

 

A natural next evolution of the detection would be to extend it to the "impossible travel" scenario.

These queries are simple to set up and are good to go to serve as new analytics rule on the solution, don’t you think?

Let me know what other scenarios you would like to see 😊

 

Thoughts on production readiness

SAP’s Audit Log Service is widely adopted across the SAP BTP services and foundational to the platform.

Sentinel for SAP BTP recently went into “General Availability” state, making it good to use for anyone who doesn’t like previews 😎

To create meaningful detections based on the SAP BTP audit log at minimum other sources, such as the Authorization and Trust Management service (XSUAA) must be considered. Enriching your threat signals with indicators from the rest of your IT landscape gets you from "SAP-security-acolyte"🧑🏻‍🏫 to master of disaster🥷🏼.

The built-in Sentinel for SAP playbooks use SAP BTP public APIs for automatic remediation. See the user API documentation for disabling users here.

 

Final words

Constantly staying ahead of attackers all the time is impossible. However, putting up a fight so they move on without doing more serious damage or at least being automatically informed about the incident puts you back in the driver’s seat.

The Sentinel for SAP BTP solution enables you to bring the SAP BTP audit log information for cross-correlation with your wider IT landscape to the Microsoft SIEM solution Sentinel. Furthermore, it powers automatic remediations like user block, password reset, and more.

For true confidence in drastic actions like blocking users, you require signals from as many sources as possible. Think beyond the SAP boundary and towards your complete IT landscape: Devices, endpoints, and suspicious logins etc. All of those touchpoints leave a trail of your attacker long before they reach SAP BTP, because of the prior phishing attempts or lateral movement etc. Have a look at Defender XDR for further info.

What detections are you running for your BTP landscape? Let the community know so we can learn from each other’s security practices.

Cheers

Martin

entry.title: Re: The use of "Bank" role in BP and Bank Hierarchy in S/...
entry.content:
Hi @EitaroYoshikawa, Thanks for the question! The bank hierarchy function in the Manage Bank Accounts - Bank Hierarchy View app allows customers to view banks in a hierarchical structure. This allows for a clear understanding of the relationship between different banks and bank groups. The hierarchy is constructed using business partners. And for this hierarchy to work, you have to assign banks with risk business partners and the role "Bank" (TR0703). If a bank is not assigned to a business partner with this role, it will not appear in the hierarchy. For more information about bank hierarchy, you can find additional details here. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/186460fdc35a4b64a713da9bb00deb1e/3fcbcc54adea6374e10000000a44538d.html?locale=en-US

Regards,

Amy

entry.title: Самый высокий реферальный код Bybit: 72963
entry.content: Ваш самый высокий реферальный код Btbit указан как 72963. При регистрации на бирже Bybit по этому коду необходимо в поле «Пригласительный код» или «Реферальный код» написать 72963. Этот код действителен пожизненно и предоставляет скидку 30% на комиссию при торговле на BYBIT.

entry.title: Code de parrainage le plus élevé Bybit : 72963
entry.content: Votre code de parrainage Btbit le plus élevé est indiqué comme 72963. Lors de votre inscription sur l'échange Bybit en utilisant ce code, vous devez écrire 72963 dans le champ « Code d'invitation » ou « Code de parrainage ». Ce code est valable à vie et offre une réduction de commission de 30 % lors des transactions sur BYBIT.
